title: Anthony Head Remembered by Buffy Stars

blocks.content: Anthony Stewart Head had many iconic roles. Dr. Frank-n-Furter, evil sports magnate, coffee commercial romcom yearner. But he was probably best known for being the Team Dad on Buffy: the Vampire Slayer. Rupert Giles mentored his young wards, gave them tough love when needed, and unwavering support when life (or demons) had them down. News of Head’s death broke June 5, with his family telling the BBC he passed from complications from pneumonia. His partner, Sarah Fisher, died six months before him.

It’s been a tough couple years for the Scooby Gang. Besides the Buffy reboot failing to make it to Hulu, multiple actors from the show have died. Nicholas Brendon, who played Xander Harris, died in March 2026. And Michelle Trachtenberg, who came onto the show in season 5 as Dawn Summers, died in February 2025.

Sarah Michelle Gellar paid tribute to her co-star on Instagram, quoting from Buffy’s speech in “The Gift,” the season 5 finale where Buffy dies. “’Tell Giles I figured it out and I’m ok,’” Gellar wrote. “Well I don’t have it figured out and I’m not ok. But I know I’m the lucky one because I knew you. Thank you to Daisy and Emily who not only shared their dad with me, but with the world.” Gellar was joined in remembering Head by Sunnydale alums like Emma Caulfield, James Marsters, and Charisma Carpenter. Here’s what they had to say about Ripper.

While Gellar was writing her tribute, fans were sharing the many scenes the two actors had together where Giles showed Buffy love and compassion as she navigated slaying. For example, many were sharing the scene when Giles refused to blame Buffy for turning Angel evil after they slept together: “If it’s guilt you’re looking for, Buffy, I’m not your man. All you will get from me is, is my support. And my respect.”

Buffy was one of David Boreanaz’s first acting jobs, and the future Jim Rockford eulogized one of his first of many co-stars. “This is how I met Anthony, in laughter and smile always,” he wrote of a picture from season 1 of BtVS. “And this is how I will remember him always. All kindness and a soul that was inspiring to me.”

James Marsters played Spike on Buffy, starting in season 2. He and Giles would occasionally whinge together about the bloody Americans they found themselves surrounded by. Marsters shared a campy photoshoot of the pair, writing “He was an unflaggingly kind and steady presence on the set of Buffy, and the best actor in the cast. He was the best of us.”

Emma Caulfield played vengeance demon Anya starting in season 3. She shared a selfie with Head from when she visited him in London in 2011, long after the show had wrapped. “There were many of these moments with this amazing human who I was lucky enough to call my friend for 27 years,” she wrote. “You were so loved. Impossible to caption. You are so missed. Rest in peace with your beautiful Sarah.”

Charisma Carpenter played Cordelia Chase on Buffy for three years, before moving to Angel for another five. She currently hosts a Buffy rewatch podcast, so her thoughts are still very much in Sunnydale. “Tony brought life to a character who, for so many, was the father figure they needed but didn’t have at home. Fans far and wide are surely grieving, and for that, I am deeply sorry,” she wrote on Instagram. “I remember meeting him in the production office for the first time and being completely thrown off by his hippie-vibe demeanor, with a touch of punk-rock flair. He had an earring, Converse high-tops, and a pair of loose-fitting, boldly striped pants. There wasn’t a trace of Giles to be found, which was a testament to his gifts.”

Clare Kramer — who played hellgod Glorificus in season 5, and whose character was ultimately killed by Giles — shared a photo of herself with Head and Marc Blucas at a Buffy fan event. “Through Giles, you gave us bravery without fearlessness, and compassion without weakness,” she wrote. “My condolences to your girls, family, and all who had the pleasure of loving you. Rest easy, Watcher.”
